sketches.mechanism: A function with no return statement implicitly returns the singleton None, which can be tested by identity.
sketches.resonance: What says nothing still comes back as absence, recognizable and exact.

sketches.mechanism: A constrained subtype enforces its numeric range at assignment, raising Constraint_Error when a value exceeds it.
sketches.resonance: A life declared to hold only zero cannot accept even one more thing.

sketches.mechanism: The Nothing type has no values and is a subtype of every type, so a non-returning expression can stand wherever any value is expected.
sketches.resonance: The thing that never arrives can fit every shape because it never has to become one.
sketches.draft:
fun answer(): Nothing = answer()

val one: Int = answer()
val yes: Boolean = answer()
